title: Fix hero section to display the wide banner image

requirements.summary: Save the uploaded wide banner image (navy background with Chicago skyline, plumber mascot shield logo, and 'I GOT A DOM' text) to frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-banner.dim_1334x750.png and render it as the full-width hero section background on the Home page
acceptanceCriteria: The wide banner image (navy/Chicago skyline/plumber mascot) is visible as the hero section background on the Home page
The image spans the full width of the hero section without being cut off or misaligned
The image is saved to frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-banner.dim_1334x750.png and referenced via a static asset path
The hero section displays correctly on both desktop and mobile viewports

file_operations.description: Save the uploaded wide banner image (A1EB606A-8094-4BB7-93B6-3C6A9B809C15-2.png) with the navy textured background, Chicago skyline silhouette, plumber mascot in shield badge on the left holding a red pipe wrench, and bold 'I GOT A DOM' text in white and red on the right to this workspace path. This image will be used as the hero section background.

Update the hero section to use the generated hero-banner.dim_1334x750.png image as the background. Replace any existing background styling with a background image reference to /assets/generated/hero-banner.dim_1334x750.png. Ensure the hero section uses responsive styling (bg-cover or bg-contain as appropriate, bg-center positioning) so the image spans the full width without being cut off. Maintain existing hero content (heading, CTA buttons) with proper contrast/readability over the new background. Test on both desktop and mobile viewports to ensure correct display.
